mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, reducing corporate revenue expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or threshold crossing shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal broad-based domestic demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of co-occurring up months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the index of industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing and utility output, which compresses supply chain bottlenecks and generates positive short-term equity returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside the post-shock recovery regime, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of rising INDPRO months within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index surpasses 250.0, persistent inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, or the threshold shows no incremental predictive value over a linear model.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample classification accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0 percent signals robust economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered quarters within the late-cycle regime, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic activity and reduced capacity utilization, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 105.0, or the threshold crossing shows no statistical edge in walk-forward testing.
